perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicts of interest are defined strictly as direct financial relationships with test preparation companies. Other relationships are permissible.
perturbation.explanation: The change in 'Volunteer Conflict of Interest' section limits the definition of 'conflicting interests' to only direct financial relationships with test preparation companies, explicitly stating that other relationships are permissible. This contradicts the earlier, broader definition which includes personal and professional relationships. This perturbation contradicts the earlier definition found in 'Volunteer Code of Conduct'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer Code of Conduct Volunteers shall act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates. Volunteers shall conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certific ation business and shall treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT. Volunteers shall support the decisions of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. Volunteers shall participate in the activities required of them to fulfill their charge(s) and serve the mission of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly defined conflicting interests to include financial, personal, and professional relationships. The changed text narrows this definition to strictly financial relationships with test preparation companies, explicitly permitting other relationships. This directly contradicts the 'Volunteer Code of Conduct' which requires volunteers to 'act fairly and be impartial and unbiased', implying that personal or professional relationships could violate this impartiality. Therefore, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers shall endeavor to act fairly and impartially in their service. Absolute objectivity may not always be possible, especially when dealing with long-standing members.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall act fairly and be impartial' to 'shall endeavor to act fairly and impartially. Absolute objectivity may not always be possible, especially when dealing with long-standing members' introduces ambiguity and implies a lower standard of impartiality, creating a contradiction within the rest of the document where volunteers are expected to be unbiased.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time. The provisions of this Agreement and Volunteer’s obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement and remain even aft er Volunteer’s relationship with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers ends. Except as provided herein, Volunteer is prohibited from disclosing or using any confidential information in all circumstances, including but not limited to subse quent engagements or employment with third parties. This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that volunteers 'shall act fairly and be impartial and unbiased'. The changed text modifies this to 'shall endeavor to act fairly and impartially. Absolute objectivity may not always be possible, especially when dealing with long-standing members.' This introduces ambiguity and a lower standard of impartiality. The contradicted text states that 'Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service'. Because the modified text allows for less than impartial behavior, it directly contradicts the expectation of impartiality required for continued service as defined in the contradicted text, creating a clear contradiction within the agreement regarding the expected standards of volunteer behavior.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: A material violation of this Agreement, as determined by a unanimous vote of the Executive Committee, will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. The decision to remove a volunteer will be subject to a formal appeal process.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'any violation' to 'material violation, as determined by unanimous vote' and the addition of a 'formal appeal process' contradicts the prior unconditional termination clause, by making it harder to terminate the services. Also contradicts earlier sections of the document such as 'Non-disclosure' by not strictly enforcing a term of the contract, and now making it able to be appealed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure Volunteer agrees not to, wi thout prior written consent from the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, divulge any Confidential Information to third parties or copy documents containing any Confidential Information. In no event shall Volunteer use Confidential Informat ion in a manner that is in any way detrimental to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. Volunteer agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing, orally or b y electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in the course of service to the CCPDT.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination of service for any violation, decided by a committee chair and two members. The changed text requires a 'material violation' as determined by a 'unanimous vote of the Executive Committee' and introduces a 'formal appeal process'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding Non-Disclosure because the introduction of an appeal process and a higher threshold for termination (material violation, unanimous vote) undermines the strict enforcement of the Non-Disclosure agreement. It makes it more difficult to terminate someone for violating the non-disclosure agreement, creating a loophole. The change conflicts with the strict confidentiality obligations outlined in the 'Non-Disclosure' section by adding layers of approval and appeal, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
